Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use contiguous same-answer segments instead of Range(min,max) for classifications

Range(min,max) caused overlapping errors when the same classification had
different answers at different points in the video (e.g. outdoor_path at
frames 10 and 150 → Range(10,150) overlapping apartment_corridor at 30-50).

Now walks frames in sorted order and starts a new Range whenever the answer
changes or there is a gap larger than INFERENCE_STRIDE. Each segment becomes
one non-overlapping ClassificationInstance with Range(start, end).

commit.message:
fix: gap analysis float-to-decimal conversion for DynamoDB compatibility (#120)

* docs: add trial reset step to diagnostics README for test_05_gap_analysis

Adds explicit 'Step 0' to reset trial credits before running test_05_gap_analysis.
The reset function verifies that applications.used=0 before proceeding with tests,
preventing 'trial exhausted' errors during live E2E validation.

* docs: improve trial reset instructions with better error handling

- Add Option A: use run_all_tests.py built-in reset (recommended)
- Add Option B: manual API reset with error handling
- Note that run_all_tests.py automatically resets via _reset_trial()
- Better handles network connectivity issues and missing domains

* fix: use resolve_api_base() for trial reset API endpoint

Replace hardcoded URL with proper API_BASE resolution from environment
or CloudFormation stack. Allows trial reset to work with any deployed environment.

* fix: add trial reset before individual tests (--test option)

When running individual tests with --test flag, run trial reset first
to ensure credits are available. Prevents 'trial exhausted' errors and
reveals actual API credit insufficiency errors.

* fix: gap analysis float-to-decimal conversion for DynamoDB compatibility

- Convert float to Decimal in dynamo_dal_handler for DynamoDB storage
- Convert Decimal back to float in gap_analysis for JSON serialization
- Change submit_response to return 200 instead of 201
- Update tests to match new status code

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add system-level SWaP-C assessment with hierarchical BOM

Add hierarchical BOM data model (die → package → PCB → system), parametric
physical estimators for weight/volume/thermal, 6-objective SWaPCEvaluator,
generic N-objective Pareto utilities, and SWaP-C scorecard reporting.
All existing 4-objective workflows unchanged (backward compatible).

- bom.py: BOMComponent tree with recursive weight/cost/volume aggregation
- physical_estimators.py: material/package/cooling tables + estimator chain
- moo/design_space.py: create_swap_design_space() with 9 vars, 6 objectives
- moo/evaluator.py: SWaPCEvaluator wrapping DesignEvaluator + system BOM
- moo/specialist.py: swap_explorer() specialist agent
- soc_state.py: max_weight_grams, max_volume_cm3, swap_assessment fields
- pareto.py: GenericParetoPoint + N-objective front/knee functions
- swap_report.py: SWaPCScorecard, ThermalAssessment, report formatting
- 67 new tests covering all phases

commit.message:
admin: add "Add from cURL" import for STT and TTS server tabs

Adds a cURL command parser and import modal to the STT and TTS server
list tabs. Clicking "+ From cURL" opens a modal with a textarea where
the user can paste any cURL command; on "Parse & Fill Form" the URL is
split into baseUrl + path, the Authorization header is extracted, query
params and form fields (model, language, etc.) are collected, and the
inline add-server form is pre-filled with those values for final review
before saving.

The parser handles: quoted strings, backslash-newline continuations,
-H/-F/-d/--data-raw flags, URL query params, and JSON bodies.

commit.message:
Fix baseline spend to project M-1 when forecasting ahead

When the user selects a target month 2+ months beyond available data
(e.g., April 2026 with data through Feb 2026), the baseline now
projects March 2026 spend using historical MoM trends instead of
always falling back to the last actual month (Jan 2026).

4-tier fallback: (1) actual M-1 of target, (2) projected M-1 via
aggregate spend MoM trends, (3) M-1 of latest data, (4) latest month.

The metric label now shows "Projected" or "Actual" in the help tooltip
to indicate the source of the baseline value.

feat: initial scrypted-camera-api plugin with REST HTTP API

for snapshots.

27 tests passing (routing + handler integration tests with SDK mocks).

fix(tailscale): patch controlUi.allowedOrigins with tailnet HTTPS origin

When gateway.bind=loopback and tailscale.mode=serve, the browser accesses
the Control UI via https://<hostname>.<tailnet>.ts.net but the gateway
rejects it as an unknown origin.

After tailscale connects, read MagicDNSSuffix from tailscale status to
build the full HTTPS origin and patch it into gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins.
Patch is idempotent — only adds if not already present.

commit.message:
fix: blame enrichment now stores ownership insights for all qualifying files

Previously the loop held the graph lock while calling store_insight
(which re-acquires it), forcing a break after the first match to avoid
deadlock. Now collects candidates under a scoped lock, then stores
insights after releasing it.

commit.message:
Tournament games: score import, per-game predictions, and bracket UI (#77)

* Add tournament games: normalized game model, score recording, and game generation

Backend:
- Add core.tournament_games table with bracket_position, round, region tracking
- Add tournament format column to tournaments table
- Add TournamentGame model and SQLC queries (CRUD, list by tournament/round)
- Add TournamentGameRepository with full adapter implementation
- Add game generator that creates tournament games from bracket structure
- Extend bracket service with dual-write (select winner writes to both bracket
  and tournament_games), score recording, and game generation endpoints
- Add HTTP handlers: list games, get game, record score, generate games
- Add tournament game DTO with response mapping
- Add integration tests for repository and unit tests for generator/service/DTOs

Frontend:
- Add tournament games query keys (games, gameDetail)
- Add tournamentGame schema with Zod validation
- Add bracket service methods for game listing and score recording
- Enhance BracketGameCard and CompactGameCard with score display
- Add games section to TournamentViewPage
- Update useBracket hook with game-aware queries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix generate-recaps build: pass gameRepo to bracket.New after signature change

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Extract TournamentGameRepository, auto-derive winner from scores, and display game status

- Extract TournamentGameRepository from TournamentRepository so game
  CRUD has its own dedicated struct and wiring
- Auto-derive winner in RecordScore when no winner is set, triggering
  the full SelectWinner flow (wins, elimination, propagation)
- Use COALESCE in UpdateTournamentGameResult to preserve existing scores
  when NULL is passed (fixes score wipe on dual-write)
- Add game status field to BracketGame model, DTO, and frontend schema
- Display "Final" and "Live" status badges in bracket UI cards
- Enqueue prediction refresh in recordScoreHandler when winner is derived

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add backfill-games CLI tool to materialize historical tournament games

Adds a CLI tool that reconstructs tournament_games rows for historical
tournaments (2016-2025) by reusing the bracket builder and result
application logic. Scores are placeholder (1-0) since historical scores
were never captured. Also fixes tournamentGameToCreateParams to pass
through scores instead of hardcoding nil.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use tournament games as source of truth for bracket rendering

Replace the dual applyCurrentResults + score overlay with a single
applyTournamentGames pure function that reads winners, scores, status,
and team assignments directly from tournament game records. Falls back
to the old win/bye-counter approach when no games exist.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add ESPN score import service, school aliases, and score-authoritative winner derivation

Introduces a score import pipeline that fetches real game scores and times
from ESPN's scoreboard API and records them against tournament games. Key
changes:

- ESPN adapter: HTTP client, response parsing, school name alias mapping
- Score import service: matches ESPN games to tournament games by team pairs,
  records scores chronologically, rebuilds game index after each recording
  to handle winner corrections that propagate to later-round slots
- RecordScore now derives winners from scores and corrects mismatches
  instead of rejecting them, making the import authoritative over
  hand-entered data
- Score import worker for polling during live tournaments
- Backfill-games safety: refuse to regenerate if games have recorded results

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove obsolete test for inconsistent winner/score rejection

RecordScore now auto-derives the winner from scores rather than
rejecting inconsistent combinations, making this test invalid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add per-game prediction system for real-time tournament updates

Instead of regenerating predictions only when an entire round completes,
the simulator now locks in decided games immediately. After each game goes
final, predictions regenerate with that outcome locked in and remaining
games simulated — enabling real-time prediction updates during the tournament.

Key changes:
- New decided_game_count column on prediction_batches (migration)
- Game-aware matchup generator that deterministically resolves decided games
- BuildDecidedByTeamPair / ComputeThroughRoundFromGames helpers
- RunGameAware service method used by the core compute worker
- Standings dedup switched from through_round to decided_game_count

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add bracket game scores and improved tournament view UI

Includes score display on bracket games, updated frontend bracket
rendering, and DTO changes to support slot scores.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix TypeScript build errors in bracket test helpers

Add required `status` default to makeGame helpers — Zod's
`.optional().default()` produces a non-optional output type, so
manually constructed BracketGame objects need the field set.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add completed_at to tournament games for prediction timeline

Records when a game result was finalized. Set automatically by
UpdateGameResult when status transitions to 'final' (uses NOW() on
first write, preserves existing value on re-record). Cleared on
ClearGameResult. Enables using real timestamps on the prediction
bump chart x-axis instead of just decided_game_count.

For historical games, completed_at can be backfilled by estimating
from scheduled_at + average game duration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add predictive warp drop before atmospheric entry

Uses time-to-periapsis from orbital elements to limit warp speed
when the orbit intersects atmosphere, preventing the ship from
phasing through during on-rails propagation. Gradually steps down
through warp levels as periapsis approaches (10-frame safety buffer).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent SIGPIPE crash and port collision with multiple IDA instances (#271)

* fix: ignore SIGPIPE to prevent IDA crash on client disconnect

IDA's embedded Python may not preserve CPython's default SIG_IGN for
SIGPIPE. When an MCP client disconnects while the HTTP server is writing
a response, the unhandled SIGPIPE kills the entire IDA process. Set
SIG_IGN at module load before any server sockets are created.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: auto-increment port for multiple IDA instances

Remove SO_REUSEPORT which let multiple IDA instances silently bind the
same port on macOS, causing request mis-routing and SIGPIPE crashes.
Keep SO_REUSEADDR for fast server restarts. Add port auto-increment
loop (13337-13436) so each IDA instance gets its own port.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: prevent duplicate heartbeat timeout and stale check during reconnect

- Stop heartbeat interval immediately on timeout before calling ws.close(),
  preventing the interval from firing again and re-marking sessions as error
- Stop stale check timer in onclose handler so it doesn't falsely corrupt
  session state (e.g. idle sessions restored after restart) during reconnect
- Add readyState guard in broadcast() to skip non-open WebSocket clients

commit.message:
HardFloat IEEE 754 silicon comparison: add/mul/FMA with fNToRecFN/recFNToFN

Add proper IEEE 754 I/O to HardFloat test harness and comparison script.
All HardFloat DUTs now use fNToRecFN input converters and recFNToFN output
converter for fair end-to-end comparison against Spirix's native format.

Silicon Fmax results (Colorlight 5A-75B, ECP5-25F, seed=4):
- HF add:  88 MHz (Spirix 95 MHz, +8%,  618 vs 1050 LUT4)
- HF mul:  65 MHz (Spirix 115 MHz, +77%, 94 vs 786 LUT4)
- HF FMA:  47 MHz (Spirix 63 MHz, +34%, 1128 vs 2057 LUT4)

header/footer margins.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(csharp): add C# AST-level analysis helpers, test fixtures, and expand scanner coverage

Add three new C# analysis helper modules:
- async_task_handles_csharp.py: detects unawaited Task/ValueTask handles,
  fire-and-forget async calls without proper error propagation, and
  missing ConfigureAwait patterns in library code
- resource_lifecycle_csharp.py: tracks IDisposable/IAsyncDisposable
  resource lifecycle — detects missing using/await using blocks,
  disposed-then-accessed objects, and Stream/DbConnection leak patterns
- type_narrowing_csharp.py: validates C# pattern matching and type
  narrowing — catches stale is/as checks after reassignment, incorrect
  null-forgiving operator usage, and switch expression exhaustiveness gaps

Add C# test suite fixtures:
- buggy/ResourceLifecycleBuggy.cs and clean/ResourceLifecycleClean.cs
  for IDisposable lifecycle regression testing
- buggy/TypeNarrowingBuggy.cs and clean/TypeNarrowingClean.cs for
  pattern-matching and type-check regression testing
- tests/test_helper_scanners.py: pytest harness exercising all three
  new C# helpers against buggy/clean fixture pairs

Expand existing modules:
- ubs-csharp.sh: wire in the three new AST helpers, add ripgrep
  heuristic patterns for unawaited tasks and disposed-after-use
- ubs-ruby.sh: add detection patterns for new Ruby resource categories
- ubs meta-runner: register C# helpers in module dispatch table and
  add async_task_handles_csharp.py to HELPER_ASSETS and HELPER_CHECKSUMS
- update_checksums.py: include all new helper files in SHA-256 manifest
  (async_task_handles, resource_lifecycle, type_narrowing for C#)
- test-suite/manifest.json: add expected-result entries for all new
  C# buggy/clean fixture pairs
- test-suite/run_all.sh: include csharp/tests in test runner
- test-suite/python/tests/test_resource_helper.py: expand Python
  resource lifecycle tests with additional edge-case coverage
- AGENTS.md, README.md, test-suite/README.md, csharp/README.md:
  update documentation to reflect new C# helper capabilities

commit.message:
fix: quote make_latest to prevent openclaw release from hijacking latest (#496)

The softprops/action-gh-release action requires make_latest to be the
string "false", not a YAML boolean. With bare false, GitHub auto-promoted
the openclaw binary release to "latest", causing install.sh to resolve
version openclaw-v3.1.9 instead of v3.1.9 and fail npm install.
